original_abstract: Tracking multiple objects in videos relies on modeling the spatial-temporal interactions of the objects. In this paper, we propose a solution named TransMOT, which leverages powerful graph transformers to efficiently model the spatial and temporal interactions among the objects. TransMOT effectively models the interactions of a large number of objects by arranging the trajectories of the tracked objects as a set of sparse weighted graphs, and constructing a spatial graph transformer encoder layer, a temporal transformer encoder layer, and a spatial graph transformer decoder layer based on the graphs. TransMOT is not only more computationally efficient than the traditional Transformer, but it also achieves better tracking accuracy. To further improve the tracking speed and accuracy, we propose a cascade association framework to handle low-score detections and long-term occlusions that require large computational resources to model in TransMOT. The proposed method is evaluated on multiple benchmark datasets including MOT15, MOT16, MOT17, and MOT20, and it achieves state-of-the-art performance on all the datasets.
humanized_abstract: Object tracking from videos has drawn increasing attention in recent years and most of the works focus on how to model the spatial-temporal relationships between objects. In this work, we propose a novel model, named as TransMOT, which adopts graph transformers to model object relationships. In particular, the tracking trajectories of multiple objects are constructed as a set of sparse weighted graphs, and then adopted to build up a spatial graph transformer encoder layer, a temporal transformer encoder layer and a spatial graph transformer decoder layer to achieve efficient and effective object relationships modeling. Compared with the traditional Transformer, our proposed TransMOT has higher computational efficiency while maintaining competitive performance. To further improve the real-time performance and tracking accuracy, the cascade association framework is introduced to deal with low-score detections and handle the long-term occlusions that needs to consume a lot of computational resources in TransMOT. In the experiments, the proposed model achieves the state-of-the-art performances on MOT15, MOT16, MOT17 and MOT20 benchmarks datasets.
